page: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the n2 family. It features 8 vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores with hyper-threading (2 threads per core) on a dedicated Intel Xeon x86_64 processor running at 2.8 GHz. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, providing 4.0 GB of memory per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

Benchmark results place the n2-standard-8 mostly in the average tier for general compute tasks. It achieves a single-core stress-ng score of 1404.09 and a multi-core score of 7398.32. Memory bandwidth performance is stable, with cached small block reads reaching 413.79 GB/sec and uncached large block writes at 37.18 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core performance fall into the bottom 25% tier, indicating potential limitations under highly parallelized, compute-heavy workloads.

Given its balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is designed for general-purpose workloads such as static web serving, where it achieves a throughput of 6.9 GB/sec, and database operations. It is also suitable for software compilation and Redis caching, delivering 7.12 million SET operations per second. The lack of bundled local storage and GPUs makes it a cost-efficient choice for standard application hosting and moderate business logic processing that does not require specialized hardware accelerators.
description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine configured with 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM. Powered by a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon x86_64 processor with 4 physical cores and hyper-threading, this instance delivers balanced compute and memory resources. It does not include bundled local storage or GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities across standard operations, including a Passmark CPU score of 8292.45 and a static web serving throughput of 6.9 GB/sec. However, multi-threaded performance in tasks like bzip3 decompression and Geekbench multi-core testing is relatively weak. This server is best suited for general-purpose workloads, including static web serving, software compilation, database operations, and Redis caching.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is a general-purpose server featuring 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, delivering balanced performance for standard web serving and database workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform n2-standard-8 is an x86_64 general-purpose server with 8 vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors for standard web serving, database operations, and application hosting.
bullet_points: 8 vCPUs and 32.0 GB of RAM on a dedicated 2.8 GHz Intel Xeon processor
Balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio suitable for general-purpose workloads
Delivers 6.9 GB/sec static web serving throughput and 7.12 million Redis SET operations per second
No bundled local storage or GPU accelerators
Best suited for web serving, database operations, and software compilation
